author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVIII. 11-14.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and declared unto the chief priests all the things that were done.And when they had assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers, saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole Him away while we slept.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if this come to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the sake of these soldiers that earthquake took place, in order to dismay them, and that the testimony might come from them, which accordingly was the result.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the report was thus free from suspicion, as proceeding from the guards themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of the signs some were displayed publicly to the world, others privately to those present on the spot; publicly for the world was the darkness, privately the appearance of the angel, the earthquake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When then they came and showed it (for truth shines forth, being proclaimed by its adversaries), they again gave money, that they might say, as it is expressed, “that His disciples came and stole Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How did they steal Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: O most foolish of all men!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because of the clearness and conspicuousness of the truth, they are not even able to make up a falsehood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed what they said was highly incredible, and the falsehood had not even speciousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For how, I ask, did the disciples steal Him, men poor and unlearned, and not venturing so much as to show themselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? was not a seal put upon it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? were there not so many watchmen, and soldiers, and Jews stationed round it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? did not those men suspect this very thing, and take thought, and break their rest, and continue anxious about it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore moreover did they steal it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they might feign the doctrine of the resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how should it enter their minds to feign such a thing, men who were well content to be hidden and to live?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how could they remove the stone that was made sure? how could they have escaped the observation of so many?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, though they had despised death, they would not have attempted without purpose, and fruitlessly to venture in defiance of so many who were on the watch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that moreover they were timorous, what they had done before showed clearly, at least, when they saw Him seized, all rushed away from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then at that time they did not dare so much as to stand their ground when they saw Him alive, how when He was dead could they but have feared such a number of soldiers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? was it to burst open a door?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was it that one should escape notice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A great stone lay upon it, needing many hands to move it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They were right in saying, “So the last error shall be worse than the first,”making this declaration against themselves, for that, when after so much mad conduct they ought to have repented, they rather strive to outdo their former acts, feigning absurd fictions, and as, when He was alive, they purchased His blood, so when He was dead and risen again, they again by money were striving to undermine the evidence of His resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou mark, I pray thee, how by their own doings they are caught everywhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they had not come to Pilate, nor asked for the guard, they would have been more able to act thus impudently, but as it was, not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed, as though they were laboring to stop their own mouths, even so did they all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the disciples had not strength to watch with Him, and that, though upbraided by Him, how could they have ventured upon these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did they not steal Him before this, but when ye were come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they had been minded to do this, they would have done it, when the tomb was not yet guarded on the first night, when it was to be done without danger, and in security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was on the Sabbath that they came and begged of Pilate to have the watch, and kept guard, but during the first night none of these was present by the sepulchre.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what mean also the napkins that were stuck on with the myrrh; for Peter saw these lying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they had been disposed to steal, they would not have stolen the body naked, not because of dishonoring it only, but in order not to delay and lose time in stripping it, and not to give them that were so disposed opportunity to awake and seize them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Especially when it was myrrh, a drug that adheres so to the body, and cleaves to the clothes, whence it was not easy to take the clothes off the body, but they that did this needed much time, so that from this again, the tale of the theft is improbable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? did they not know the rage of the Jews? and that they would vent their anger on them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what profit was it at all to them, if He had not risen again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So these men, being conscious that they had made up all this tale, gave money, and said, “Say ye these things, and we will persuade the governor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they desire that the report should be published, fighting in vain against the truth; and by their endeavors to obscure it, by these even against their will they occasioned it to appear clearly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed even this establishes the resurrection, the fact I mean of their saying, that the disciples stole Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the language of men confessing, that the body was not there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore they confess the body was not there, but the stealing it is shown to be false and incredible, by their watching by it, and by the seals, and by the timidity of the disciples, the proof of the resurrection even hence appears incontrovertible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, these shameless and audacious men, although there were so many things to stop their mouths, “Say ye,” these are their words, “and we will persuade, and will secure you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou all depraved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Pilate, for he was persuaded? the soldiers? the Jewish people?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But marvel not, if money prevailed over soldiers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if with His disciple it showed its might to be so great, much more with these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And this saying is commonly reported,” it is said, “until this day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou again the disciples’ love of truth, how they are not ashamed of saying even this, that such a report prevailed against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, and some worshipped, and some when they saw Him doubted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This seems to me to be the last appearance in Galilee, when He sent them forth to baptize.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if “some doubted,” herein again admire their truthfulness, how they conceal not even their shortcomings up to the last day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, even these are assured by their sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He unto them, when He seeth them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “All power is given unto me in heaven and on earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again He speaketh to them more after the manner of man, for they had not yet received the spirit, which was able to raise them on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Go ye, make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost: teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you;”giving the one charge with a view to doctrine, the other concerning commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of the Jews He makes no mention, neither brings forward what had been done, nor upbraids Peter with his denial, nor any one of the others with their flight, but having put into their hands a summary of the doctrine, that expressed by the form of baptism, commands them to pour forth over the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After that, because he had enjoined on them great things, to raise their courage, He says, “Lo!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou His own proper power again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how those other things also were spoken for condescension?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not with those men only did He promise to be, but also with all that believe after them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For plainly the apostles were not to remain here unto “the end of the world;” but he speaks to the believers as to one body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not, saith He, of the difficulty of the things: for “I am with you,” who make all things easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This He said to the prophets also in the Old Testament continually, as well to Jeremiah objecting his youth,as to Mosesand Ezekielshrinking from the office, “I am with you,” this here also to these men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark, I pray thee, the excellence of these, for the others, when sent to one nation, often excused themselves, but these said nothing of the sort, though sent to the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He reminds them also of the consummation, that He may draw them on more, and that they may look not at the present dangers only, but also at the good things to come that are without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the irksome things, saith He, that ye will undergo are finished together with the present life, since at least even this world itself shall come to an end, but the good things which ye shall enjoy remain immortal, as I have often told you before.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus having invigorated and roused their minds, by the remembrance of that day, He sent them forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that day to them that live in good works is to be desired, even as on the other hand to those in sin, it is terrible as to the condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us not fear only, and shudder, but let us change too, while there is opportunity, and let us rise out of our wickedness, for we can, if we be willing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if before grace many did this, much more after grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what grievous things are we enjoined? to cleave mountains asunder? to fly into the air? or to cross the Tuscan sea?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but a way of life so easy, as not so much as to want any instruments, but a soul and purpose only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what instruments had these apostles, who effected such things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they not go about with one vestment and unshod? and they got the better of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is difficult of the injunctions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have no enemy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hate no man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Speak ill of no man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, the opposites of these things are the greater hardships.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He said, you reply, Throw away thy money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is this then the grievous thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the first place, He did not command, but advised it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet even if it were a command, what is it grievous not to carry about burdens and unseasonable cares?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But oh covetousness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All things are become money; for this cause all things are turned upside down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If anyone declares another happy, he mentions this; should he pronounce him wretched, hence is derived the description of wretchedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all reckonings are made on this account, how such an one gets rich, how such an one gets poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Should it be military service, should it be marriage, should it be a trade, should it be what you will that any man takes in hand, he does not apply to what is proposed, until he see these riches are coming in rapidly upon him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this shall we not meet together and consult how we shall drive away this pest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall we not regard with shame the good deeds of our fathers? of the three thousand, of the five thousand, who had all things common?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is the profit of this present life, when we do not use it for our future gain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long do ye not enslave the mammon that hath enslaved you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long are ye slaves of money?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long have ye no love for liberty, and do not rend in pieces the bargains of covetousness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But while, if ye should have become slaves of men, you do all things, if any one should promise you liberty; yet being captives of covetousness, ye do not so much as consider how ye may be delivered from this bitter bondage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the one were nothing terrible, the other is the most bitter tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider how great a price Christ paid for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He shed His own blood; He gave up Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But ye, even after all this, are grown supine; and the most grievous thing of all is, that ye even take delight in the slavery, ye luxuriate in the dishonor, and that, from which ye ought to flee, is become an object of desire to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since it is right not only to lament and to blame, but also to correct, let us see from what cause this passion and this evil have become an object of desire to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then, whence hath this come to be an object of desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, thou sayest, it makes me to be in honor and in security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In what kind of security, I pray thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the confidence, not to suffer hunger, nor cold, not to be harmed, not to be despised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou then, if we promise thee this security, refrain from being rich?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if it is for this that riches are an object of desire, if it be in your power to have security without these, what need hast thou of these any more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And how is it possible,” thou sayest, “for one who is not rich to attain to this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, how is it possible (for I say the opposite thing) if one is rich?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is necessary to flatter many, both rulers and subjects, and to entreat countless numbers, and to be a base slave, and to be in fear and trembling, and to regard with suspicion the eyes of the envious, and to fear the tongues of false accusers, and the desires of other covetous men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But poverty is not like this, but altogether the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is a place of refuge and security, a calm harbor, a wrestling ground, and school of exercise to learn self-command, an imitation of the life of angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear these things, as many as are poor; or rather also, as many as desire to be rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not poverty that is the thing to be feared, but the not being willing to be poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Account poverty to be nothing to fear, and it will not be to thee a matter for fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is this fear in the nature of the thing, but in the judgment of feeble-minded men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather, I am even ashamed that I have occasion to say so much concerning poverty, to show that it is nothing to be feared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou practise self-command, it is even a fountain to thee of countless blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if any one were to offer thee sovereignty, and political power, and wealth, and luxury, and then having set against them poverty, were to give thee thy choice to take which thou wouldest, thou wouldest straightway seize upon poverty, if indeed thou knewest the beauty thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I know that many laugh, when these things are said; but we are not troubled but we require you to stay, and soon ye will give judgment with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to me poverty seems like some comely, fair, and well-favored damsel, but covetousness like some monster shaped woman, some Scylla or Hydra, or some other like prodigies feigned by fabulous writers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For bring not forward, I pray thee, them that accuse poverty, but them that have shone thereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nurtured in this, Elias was caught up in that blessed assumption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this Eliseus shone; with this John; with this all the apostles; but with the other, Ahab, Jezebel, Gehazi, Judas, Nero, Caiaphas, were condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if it please you, let us not look to those only that have been glorious in poverty, but let us observe the beauty itself of this damsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed her eye is clear and piercing, having nothing turbid in it, like the eye of covetousness, which is at one time full of anger, at another sated with pleasure, at another troubled by incontinence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the eye of poverty is not like this, but mild, calm, looking kindly on all, meek, gentle, hating no man, shunning no man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For where there are riches, there is matter for enmity, and for countless wars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The mouth again of the other is full of insults, of a certain haughtiness, of much boasting, cursing, deceit; but the mouth and the tongue of this are sound, filled with continual thanksgiving, blessing, words of gentleness, of affection, of courtesy, of praise, of commendation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou wouldest see also the proportion of her members, she is of a goodly height, and far loftier than wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if many flee from her, marvel not at it, for indeed so do fools from the rest of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the poor man, thou wilt say, is insulted by him that is rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again thou art declaring to me the praise of poverty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who, I pray thee, is blessed, the insulter, or the insulted?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is manifest that it is the insulted person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But then, the one, covetousness, urges to insult the other; poverty persuades to endure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But the poor man suffers hunger,” thou wilt say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Paul also suffered hunger, and was in famine.“But he has no rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither “had the Son of Man where to lay His head.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how far the praises of poverty have proceeded, and where it places thee, to what men it leads thee on, and how it makes thee a follower of the Lord?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If it were good to have gold, Christ, who have the unutterable blessings, would have given this to His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now so far from giving it them, He forbad them to have it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Peter also, so far from being ashamed of poverty, even glories in it, saying, “Silver and gold have I none; but what I have give I thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And who of you would not have desired to utter this saying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, we all would extremely, perhaps some one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then throw away thy silver, throw away thy gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And if I throw it away, thou wilt say, shall I receive the power of Peter?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, what made Peter blessed, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was it indeed to have lifted up the lame man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but the not having these riches, this procured him Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of those that wrought these miracles, many fell into hell, but they, who did those good things, attained a kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this you may learn even of Peter himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there were two things that he said, “Silver and gold have I none;” and, “In the name of Jesus Christ rise up and walk.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which sort of thing then made Him glorious and blessed, the raising up the lame man, or the casting away his money?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this you may learn from the Master of the conflicts Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then doth He Himself say to the rich man seeking eternal life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “raise up the lame,” but, “Sell thy goods, and give to the poor, and come and follow me, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Peter again said not, “Behold, in Thy name we cast out devils;” although he was casting them out, but, “Behold, we have forsaken all and followed Thee what shall we have?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Christ again, in answering this apostle, said not, “If any man raise up the lame,” but, “Whosoever hath forsaken houses or lands, shall receive an hundredfold in this world, and shall inherit everlasting life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us also then emulate this man, that we may not be confounded, but may with confidence stand at the judgment seat of Christ; that we may win Him to be with us, even as He was with His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He will be with us, like as He was with them, if we are willing to follow them, and to be imitators of their life and conversation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in consequence of these things God crowns, and commends men, not requiring of thee to raise the dead, or to cure the lame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not these things make one to be like Peter, but the casting away one’s goods, for this was the apostles’ achievement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But dost thou not find it possible to cast them away?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the first place, I say, it is possible; but I compel thee not, if thou art not willing, nor constrain thee to it; but this I entreat, to spend at least a part on the needy, and to seek for thyself nothing more than is necessary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shall we both live our life here without trouble, and in security, and enjoy eternal life; unto which God grant we all may attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might, together with the Father and the Holy Ghost, now and always, and world without end.
